title: Inbound Lead Magnet Agent
problem: Marketing teams want to convert inbound form fills into personalized, high-value content immediately — but manually creating custom deliverables at scale is impossible.
solution: Form submission triggers an agent that researches the prospect's company and role, generates a personalized PDF deliverable, delivers it by email, and logs the enriched lead in HubSpot.
workflowSteps: Form submission event triggers the workflow
Agent enriches lead data: web search for company news, LinkedIn company profile
Generates personalized PDF report/guide tailored to company size and industry
Sends PDF to prospect via email within minutes of form fill
Logs enriched lead record (company context, generated asset) to HubSpot CRM
keyInsights: The CRM write must be idempotent — form re-submissions must not create duplicate leads. This is the same idempotency-key pattern from invoice-ops-hitl, applied to a marketing automation context.

relatedExams.why: Applications and Integration — CRM integration, webhook triggers

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([Form Submission]) --> B[Enrich Lead Data]
  B --> C1[Web Search: Company News]
  B --> C2[LinkedIn: Company Profile]
  C1 & C2 --> D[Generate Custom PDF]
  D --> E([Email to Prospect])
  D --> F([HubSpot CRM Write + Idempotency Key])
architectureNotes: Inbound form submissions trigger the agent. It scores the lead using a classification model, then branches: high-fit leads get a personalised nurture email drafted by an LLM and queued for human approval; low-fit leads receive an automated acknowledgement. CRM records are created or updated idempotently. A daily digest surfaces leads awaiting human action.

tools: Claude classification (industry, company size, intent signals)

failureModes.mode: Lead enrichment returns stale company data (M&A, rebranding)
failureModes.mitigation: TTL cache on enrichment data (7 days); flag low-confidence enrichments for human QA; use multiple enrichment sources and take majority.

failureModes.mode: Personalised email contains wrong company or role details
failureModes.mitigation: Template sanity check: verify all {{variables}} are populated before send; staging send to internal inbox for visual QA on first 50 leads.

failureModes.mode: CRM duplicate created for returning lead
failureModes.mitigation: Upsert by email (dedupe key); check existing records before insert; merge logic for same-company different-contact scenarios.

failureModes.mode: High-fit leads missed due to miscalibrated scoring model
failureModes.mitigation: Sales feedback loop: reps mark leads as 'should have been prioritised'; retrain scoring prompt monthly; track lead-to-opportunity conversion by score tier.

scalingConsiderations: Enrichment cost: Clearbit ~$0.05–0.10/lead; for high-volume (10,000+ leads/month), gate enrichment behind score threshold to avoid enriching all spam.
Email send timing: personalised email within 5 minutes of form submit shows 3× higher open rates; prioritise queue processing for hot leads.
CRM sync latency: Salesforce API rate limits at 100 calls/s; use bulk upsert endpoint for batch processing; real-time upsert for high-priority leads only.

integrations.note: Webhook on form submit (Typeform, HubSpot forms, custom)
Lead enrichment — company size, industry, tech stack
Creates/updates lead record with score and context
Notifies sales rep for high-score leads
